name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi
description: An initiative by the Government of India that provides up to ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments to all small and marginal landholding farmer families as direct income support.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: ₹6,000 per annum distributed directly to bank accounts in 3 equal installments.

name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
hindiName: आयुष्मान भारत - प्रधानमंत्री जन आरोग्य योजना
description: The largest health assurance scheme in the world which aims to provide a health cover of ₹5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to poor and vulnerable families.
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
benefits: Cashless health cover of up to ₹500,000 per family per year at empaneled hospitals.

name: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana
description: A scheme to provide collateral-free micro loans up to ₹10 Lakhs to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises to scale business operations and promote entrepreneurship.
benefits: Collateral-free business development funds categorized from ₹50,000 up to ₹1,000,000.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
description: A small deposit savings scheme promoted under the 'Beti Bachao Beti Padhao' campaign, explicitly meant to secure the financial future of a girl child including higher studies and marriage.
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
benefits: High-interest savings account with absolute income tax exemptions under Section 80C.

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana
description: An initiative by the Government of India in which affordable housing is provided to the urban and rural poor with financial interest subsidies for building permanent pucca houses.
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
benefits: Financial interest subsidy up to ₹2.67 lakhs for house construction or purchase loans.
requiredDocuments: Affidavit declaring no existing pucca house

name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana
description: A skill certification scheme aimed at enabling a large number of Indian youth to take up industry-relevant, placement-linked vocational skill training that helps secure a better livelihood.
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
benefits: Free industry skill certification courses along with direct monetary rewards upon passing assessments.

name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNidhi
description: A special micro-credit facility scheme for providing affordable working capital loans to urban and rural street vendors to resume their businesses and secure self-reliance.
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
benefits: Initial working capital loan up to ₹10,000, scaling to ₹20,000 and ₹50,000 on timely repayments with 7% interest subsidy.

name: Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme
description: A comprehensive welfare scheme intended to facilitate delivery of assistive devices and free vocational training to persons with physical disabilities.
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
benefits: Free aids, customized assistive devices, prosthetics distribution, and specialized educational setups.

name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana
description: A comprehensive crop insurance scheme providing complete financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage arising out of unforeseen natural calamities and pests.
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
benefits: Full insurance cover against crop failures with extremely low premium structures (1.5% to 5%).

name: Stand-Up India Scheme
description: A focused initiative promoting entrepreneurship at the grassroots level specifically targeting Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), and Women entrepreneurs.
benefits: Bank loans ranging between ₹10 Lakhs and ₹1 Crore for setting up a greenfield enterprise.

name: Mukhyamantri Maiya Samman Yojana
description: The Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Maiya Samman Yojana is a state-run social welfare initiative providing ₹2,500 per month (₹30,000 annually) directly to eligible women through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). It aims to promote financial independence, elevate socio-economic status, and help underprivileged women meet their daily household and personal expenses.Key Details & EligibilityFinancial Benefit: ₹2,500 per month deposited into an Aadhaar-seeded bank account.Age Limit: Women between the ages of 18 and 50 years.Residency: Must be a permanent resident of Jharkhand.Income Cap: The applicant's family must have an annual income of less than ₹8 lakh.Inclusions: Both married and unmarried women, as well as widows, who are registered on a valid ration card are eligible.Exclusions: The scheme does not apply to regular government employees, income-tax payers, or beneficiaries of other state social security pension schemes.
requiredDocuments: Passport Size Photograph and Filled Self-Declaration Form
